governance-and-oversight.name: Board Technology Risk Oversight
governance-and-oversight.description: Formal board-level oversight of technology risk through dedicated agenda time, documented challenge of management, and formal acceptance of the institution's technology risk posture.
keyActivities: Establish technology risk as a standing board agenda item with dedicated discussion time
Require board members to formally challenge management technology risk presentations
Document board decisions on technology risk matters including budget approvals and risk acceptance
Maintain board minutes evidencing substantive deliberation on technology risk

maturity.basic: Technology risk is discussed at board level ad-hoc, typically bundled under operational risk with no dedicated agenda time
maturity.mature: Standing quarterly agenda item with structured reporting template, documented challenge and action tracking, formal risk acceptance records
maturity.advanced: Real-time technology risk dashboard for board consumption, pre-meeting briefings, formal board technology risk appetite review cycle with quantified risk metrics

governance-and-oversight.name: Technology Risk Committee
governance-and-oversight.description: Dedicated board-level or senior management committee with defined terms of reference for overseeing technology risk management, meeting at least quarterly with documented minutes and escalation authority.
keyActivities: Establish committee with formal terms of reference covering technology risk oversight
Ensure committee membership includes members with technology risk expertise
Maintain structured meeting cadence with documented minutes and action items
Define escalation pathways from committee to full board for material technology risk matters

maturity.basic: Technology risk discussed within existing audit or risk committee with no dedicated terms of reference
maturity.mature: Dedicated technology risk committee with formal TOR, quarterly meetings, competency requirements for members, and documented escalation framework
maturity.advanced: Committee supported by dedicated secretariat, real-time risk feeds, formal liaison with operational security teams, and annual effectiveness review

governance-and-oversight.name: Board Technology Competency Program
governance-and-oversight.description: Structured program ensuring board members and senior management maintain adequate competency in technology risk matters, including regular briefings, training, and competency assessments.
keyActivities: Conduct annual technology risk competency assessment for all board members
Deliver structured briefings on emerging technology risks, cyber threats, and regulatory developments
Provide targeted training for newly appointed directors on institutional technology risk landscape
Maintain training records and competency evidence for BNM examination readiness

maturity.basic: Ad-hoc briefings to the board on technology topics with no formal competency assessment
maturity.mature: Annual competency assessment, structured training calendar, individual development plans for directors, and competency matrix maintained by company secretary
maturity.advanced: Continuous competency development with external expert sessions, tabletop cyber exercises for the board, and benchmarking against industry best practices

governance-and-oversight.name: Technology Risk Appetite Framework
governance-and-oversight.description: Board-approved risk appetite statement defining acceptable technology risk levels with quantified tolerance thresholds, integrated into enterprise risk management and reviewed annually.
keyActivities: Define quantified risk appetite thresholds for technology risk categories (availability, integrity, confidentiality, cyber)
Integrate technology risk appetite into the enterprise risk management framework
Obtain formal board approval and annual review of the technology risk appetite statement
Monitor actual risk exposure against appetite thresholds with escalation when breached

maturity.basic: Generic qualitative risk appetite statement that mentions technology risk without specific thresholds or measurable tolerance levels
maturity.mature: Quantified risk appetite with specific KRI thresholds per risk category, board-approved annually, with automated monitoring and breach escalation
maturity.advanced: Dynamic risk appetite framework linked to real-time risk metrics, scenario-based stress testing of appetite levels, and formal linkage to capital adequacy planning

governance-and-oversight.name: Senior Management Accountability
governance-and-oversight.description: Clear accountability framework assigning named senior management owners for all technology risk domains, with documented CISO mandate, direct board reporting line, and adequate resourcing.
keyActivities: Appoint a CISO with formal mandate and direct reporting line to the board or board committee
Maintain a senior management accountability matrix mapping risk domains to named owners
Ensure CISO has adequate budget, headcount, and organisational independence
Document and enforce escalation procedures from CISO to board for material incidents

maturity.basic: CISO role exists but reports through IT department with no direct board access and limited budget autonomy
maturity.mature: CISO with formal board reporting mandate, dedicated budget, independence from IT operations, and documented accountability matrix for all technology risk domains
maturity.advanced: CISO as member of senior management committee with strategic input, supported by a fully staffed second-line technology risk function, and annual mandate review by the board

governance-and-oversight.name: Technology Risk Management Function
governance-and-oversight.description: Independent second-line technology risk function with adequate staffing, competency, and organisational independence to challenge first-line technology operations.
keyActivities: Establish a dedicated technology risk function independent from IT operations
Define and maintain the function charter with clear roles, responsibilities, and reporting lines
Ensure function staffing meets competency requirements across all technology risk domains
Conduct independent technology risk assessments and challenge first-line risk decisions

maturity.basic: Technology risk responsibilities distributed across IT and risk functions with no dedicated team or formal charter
maturity.mature: Dedicated technology risk team with formal charter, defined reporting lines, competency framework, and regular independent assessments across all technology domains
maturity.advanced: Fully staffed three-lines model with dedicated technology risk specialists per domain, automated risk analytics capabilities, and continuous assurance programme

risk-management.name: Technology Risk Management Framework
risk-management.description: Board-approved overarching framework defining the institution's approach to identifying, assessing, treating, monitoring, and reporting technology risks, aligned with BNM RMiT requirements and integrated with enterprise risk management.
keyActivities: Develop and maintain a comprehensive technology risk management framework aligned with RMiT PD 2025
Obtain formal board approval and conduct annual framework review
Integrate technology risk management into the enterprise risk management framework
Map framework components to specific RMiT clauses for examination readiness
Define technology risk taxonomy covering all relevant risk categories

maturity.basic: Technology risk framework exists as a standalone document with limited integration to enterprise risk management and infrequent reviews
maturity.mature: Board-approved framework fully integrated with ERM, annually reviewed, mapped to RMiT clauses, with defined risk taxonomy and clear roles and responsibilities
maturity.advanced: Living framework with real-time updates based on threat landscape changes, automated compliance mapping, and continuous alignment with international standards (NIST CSF, ISO 27001)

risk-management.name: Technology Risk Assessment
risk-management.description: Systematic identification and assessment of technology risks across all systems, processes, and third parties, using a standardised methodology with defined frequency and scope covering the full technology estate.
keyActivities: Maintain a standardised risk assessment methodology with defined criteria, scales, and scoring
Conduct periodic risk assessments covering infrastructure, applications, data, and third parties
Ensure risk assessments cover both inherent and residual risk with documented control effectiveness
Integrate risk assessment results into the technology risk register and board reporting

maturity.basic: Annual risk assessments conducted using spreadsheets with inconsistent methodology across teams
maturity.mature: Standardised risk assessment methodology executed quarterly, GRC tool-supported, covering all critical systems with automated residual risk calculation
maturity.advanced: Continuous risk assessment with automated asset discovery, real-time threat feeds informing risk scores, and predictive risk analytics

risk-management.name: Risk Treatment & Acceptance
risk-management.description: Formal process for selecting and implementing risk treatment options (mitigate, transfer, accept, avoid) with documented treatment plans, formal risk acceptance by authorised management, and tracking of residual risk.
keyActivities: Document risk treatment decisions with rationale for selected option
Maintain risk treatment plans with named owners, milestones, and target dates
Ensure formal risk acceptance is authorised at appropriate management level based on risk magnitude
Track treatment plan execution and residual risk against defined tolerance thresholds

maturity.basic: Risk treatment decisions documented informally with limited tracking of treatment plan execution
maturity.mature: Formal risk treatment workflow with defined acceptance authority matrix, treatment plan tracking in GRC tool, and periodic residual risk review
maturity.advanced: Automated risk treatment workflows with real-time progress tracking, automated escalation for overdue items, and dynamic residual risk recalculation

risk-management.name: Risk Monitoring & KRI Dashboard
risk-management.description: Continuous monitoring of technology risk through key risk indicators (KRIs) with defined thresholds, automated alerting, trend analysis, and regular reporting to senior management and the board.
keyActivities: Define KRIs for each technology risk category with green/amber/red threshold levels
Implement automated KRI data collection and dashboard reporting
Establish escalation procedures when KRI thresholds are breached
Report KRI trends to senior management monthly and to the board quarterly

maturity.basic: Limited KRIs tracked manually with quarterly reporting and no automated threshold alerting
maturity.mature: Comprehensive KRI dashboard with automated data feeds, threshold-based alerting, trend analysis, and structured reporting cadence to management and board
maturity.advanced: Real-time KRI monitoring with predictive analytics, correlation across risk categories, automated board reporting, and benchmarking against industry peers

risk-management.name: Technology Risk Register
risk-management.description: Centralised, maintained register of all identified technology risks with current risk ratings, treatment status, named owners, and linkage to controls and compliance requirements.
keyActivities: Maintain a centralised technology risk register accessible to all risk stakeholders
Ensure each risk entry has a named owner, current rating, treatment plan, and target date
Link registered risks to relevant controls, RMiT clauses, and compliance requirements
Conduct periodic register reviews to validate risk ratings and treatment plan progress

maturity.basic: Risk register maintained in spreadsheet with infrequent updates and limited stakeholder access
maturity.mature: GRC tool-based risk register with automated workflows, linkage to controls and compliance requirements, and quarterly review cycle
maturity.advanced: Dynamic risk register with real-time updates from automated scanning, auto-linkage to threat intelligence, and API integration with ITSM and SIEM for continuous validation

project-and-development.name: IT Project Governance
project-and-development.description: Governance framework for IT projects ensuring formal risk assessment, management oversight, milestone tracking, and post-implementation review for all technology initiatives.
keyActivities: Mandate formal technology risk assessment for all IT projects above defined threshold
Establish project steering committee with senior management representation
Implement stage-gate reviews at key project milestones with formal go/no-go decisions
Conduct mandatory post-implementation reviews within defined timeframes
Maintain project risk register and escalation procedures

maturity.basic: IT projects managed individually with inconsistent governance and no mandatory risk assessment or post-implementation review
maturity.mature: Standardised project governance framework with mandatory risk assessments, steering committees, stage-gates, and post-implementation reviews for all significant projects
maturity.advanced: Portfolio-level project governance with integrated risk views, automated milestone tracking, lessons-learned database, and predictive project risk analytics

project-and-development.name: Systems Development Lifecycle Framework
project-and-development.description: Standardised SDLC methodology covering requirements management, design, coding, testing, UAT, and deployment with security integrated at every phase.
keyActivities: Maintain a documented SDLC methodology with security requirements embedded at each phase
Enforce formal requirements management with traceability to business and regulatory needs
Mandate comprehensive testing including unit, integration, system, performance, and security testing
Require formal UAT sign-off with documented acceptance criteria before production deployment

maturity.basic: SDLC exists on paper but inconsistently followed, with limited security integration and informal testing practices
maturity.mature: Standardised SDLC with embedded security gates, automated testing pipelines, formal UAT processes, and full requirements traceability
maturity.advanced: DevSecOps pipeline with automated security scanning at every stage, infrastructure-as-code, continuous integration/deployment, and shift-left security practices

project-and-development.name: Secure Development Practices
project-and-development.description: Secure coding standards, code review processes, and static/dynamic analysis tools ensuring application security is built in rather than bolted on.
keyActivities: Enforce secure coding standards aligned with OWASP and CWE guidelines
Mandate peer code reviews with security-focused review checklists
Integrate static application security testing (SAST) into the CI/CD pipeline
Conduct dynamic application security testing (DAST) before production release

maturity.basic: Secure coding guidelines exist but are not systematically enforced; code reviews are informal
maturity.mature: Mandatory SAST/DAST in CI/CD pipeline, peer code reviews with security checklists, developer security training programme, and tracked remediation of findings
maturity.advanced: Full DevSecOps with automated SCA, secrets scanning, container security scanning, runtime application self-protection (RASP), and developer security champions programme

project-and-development.name: Change Management
project-and-development.description: Formal change management process governing all modifications to production systems, including change classification, CAB review, approval workflows, rollback procedures, and emergency change handling.
keyActivities: Classify all changes by risk level with appropriate approval authority at each level
Operate a Change Advisory Board (CAB) with defined membership and review procedures
Maintain documented rollback procedures for all production changes
Define and enforce emergency change procedures with post-implementation review
Track change success/failure rates as a key operational metric

maturity.basic: Change process exists but is inconsistently followed with limited CAB oversight and no formal emergency change procedure
maturity.mature: ITSM-based change management with risk classification, CAB governance, mandatory rollback plans, emergency change workflow, and success rate tracking
maturity.advanced: Automated change risk assessment, integrated with CI/CD pipeline, real-time change collision detection, and predictive change failure analysis

project-and-development.name: Environment Segregation
project-and-development.description: Strict separation of development, testing, staging, and production environments with controls preventing unauthorised data flow and access between environments.
keyActivities: Maintain physically or logically separated environments for development, testing, and production
Prohibit use of production data in non-production environments without formal masking or anonymisation
Restrict production access to authorised operations staff only
Implement network controls preventing direct connectivity between non-production and production environments

maturity.basic: Environments exist but share infrastructure, production data is used in testing, and access boundaries are not strictly enforced
maturity.mature: Strictly segregated environments with network controls, automated data masking for non-production use, separate access control policies, and formal promotion processes
maturity.advanced: Infrastructure-as-code environment provisioning, ephemeral test environments, zero-trust network segmentation, and automated compliance validation of environment boundaries

project-and-development.name: Source Code Protection
project-and-development.description: Controls protecting source code from unauthorised access, modification, and leakage, including version control, access restrictions, and code repository security.
keyActivities: Store all source code in access-controlled version control repositories
Enforce branch protection rules requiring code review before merge to main branches
Implement secrets scanning to prevent credentials from being committed to repositories
Restrict repository access based on role and project assignment

maturity.basic: Source code stored in version control but with broad access permissions and no branch protection or secrets scanning
maturity.mature: Access-controlled repositories with branch protection, mandatory code reviews, secrets scanning, and audit logging of all repository access
maturity.advanced: Comprehensive code security programme with signed commits, code provenance tracking, software bill of materials (SBOM), and automated supply chain security

project-and-development.name: Configuration Management
project-and-development.description: Standardised configuration baselines for all systems with change tracking, drift detection, and hardening aligned to CIS benchmarks or equivalent standards.
keyActivities: Define and maintain hardened configuration baselines for all system types (servers, network devices, databases, endpoints)
Implement automated configuration drift detection and alerting
Align baseline configurations with CIS benchmarks or equivalent hardening standards
Track all configuration changes through the change management process

maturity.basic: Configuration standards exist for some systems but are manually maintained with no drift detection
maturity.mature: Standardised baselines across all system types with automated drift detection, CIS benchmark alignment, and integration with change management
maturity.advanced: Infrastructure-as-code with immutable configurations, automated remediation of drift, continuous compliance scanning, and golden image management

it-operations.name: Capacity & Performance Management
it-operations.description: Proactive capacity planning and performance monitoring ensuring systems operate within defined thresholds and can scale to meet business demand.
keyActivities: Maintain capacity plans for all critical systems with growth projections aligned to business forecasts
Implement real-time performance monitoring with threshold-based alerting
Conduct periodic capacity reviews and trend analysis to identify potential constraints
Define and enforce performance SLAs for critical business applications

maturity.basic: Reactive capacity management with monitoring limited to basic utilisation metrics and no formal capacity planning
maturity.mature: Proactive capacity planning with automated monitoring, trend analysis, growth modelling, and defined performance SLAs for critical systems
maturity.advanced: Predictive capacity analytics with auto-scaling capabilities, machine learning-based anomaly detection, and integrated capacity-cost optimisation

it-operations.name: Patch Management
it-operations.description: Timely identification, prioritisation, testing, and deployment of security and system patches across the entire technology estate with defined SLAs based on severity.
keyActivities: Define patch deployment SLAs based on vulnerability severity (critical within 14 days, high within 30 days)
Maintain comprehensive patch inventory covering all operating systems, applications, and firmware
Test patches in non-production environment before production deployment
Track and report patch compliance rates across the technology estate
Manage exceptions through formal risk acceptance when patches cannot be applied within SLA

maturity.basic: Manual patching process with inconsistent coverage and no defined SLAs; significant patch backlog common
maturity.mature: Automated patch scanning and deployment tools, defined SLAs by severity, non-production testing, compliance dashboards, and formal exception management
maturity.advanced: Fully automated patch pipeline with zero-day response capability, virtual patching for interim mitigation, and integration with vulnerability management for risk-based prioritisation

it-operations.name: End-of-Life Management
it-operations.description: Proactive identification and management of end-of-life and end-of-support technology components with documented migration plans, compensating controls, and formal risk acceptance where legacy systems remain in use.
keyActivities: Maintain a technology lifecycle register tracking EoL/EoS dates for all hardware and software
Develop migration or replacement plans at least 12 months before end-of-support date
Implement compensating controls for legacy systems that cannot be immediately replaced
Require formal risk acceptance from senior management for any system operating beyond end-of-support

maturity.basic: EoL tracking is ad-hoc with systems frequently discovered to be unsupported only after vendor notification
maturity.mature: Comprehensive lifecycle register with proactive 12-month migration planning, compensating controls for legacy systems, and formal risk acceptance process
maturity.advanced: Automated lifecycle tracking integrated with asset management, predictive replacement budgeting, and zero-tolerance policy for unsupported systems in production

cryptography.name: Cryptographic Controls
cryptography.description: Formal cryptographic policy defining approved algorithms, key lengths, and use cases for data-at-rest and data-in-transit encryption across the institution.
keyActivities: Maintain a cryptographic policy specifying approved algorithms and minimum key lengths
Enforce encryption for all data in transit using TLS 1.2+ and data at rest using AES-256 or equivalent
Conduct periodic cryptographic inventory to identify weak or deprecated algorithms
Plan migration path for post-quantum cryptography readiness

maturity.basic: Encryption applied inconsistently with no formal policy on approved algorithms or minimum key lengths
maturity.mature: Formal cryptographic policy with approved algorithm catalogue, mandatory encryption for sensitive data at rest and in transit, and periodic cryptographic assessments
maturity.advanced: Centralised cryptographic services platform, automated discovery of unencrypted data flows, crypto-agility for algorithm migration, and post-quantum readiness planning

cryptography.name: Key & Certificate Management
cryptography.description: Full lifecycle management of cryptographic keys and digital certificates including generation, distribution, rotation, revocation, and secure destruction.
keyActivities: Implement centralised key management system with HSM-backed key generation and storage
Define key rotation schedules and enforce automated rotation where possible
Maintain certificate inventory with expiry alerting at least 60 days before expiration
Document key recovery and escrow procedures for business continuity

maturity.basic: Keys and certificates managed by individual teams with no central inventory or rotation schedule; certificate expiry outages are common
maturity.mature: Centralised KMS with HSM backing, automated certificate lifecycle management, defined rotation schedules, and inventory with expiry alerting
maturity.advanced: Fully automated certificate lifecycle with zero-touch rotation, API-driven key provisioning, ACME protocol for web certificates, and comprehensive crypto asset inventory

physical-security.name: Data Centre Physical Security
physical-security.description: Multi-layered physical access controls for data centres including biometric access, visitor management, CCTV surveillance, and security guard patrols.
keyActivities: Implement multi-factor physical access control (card + biometric) for all data centre zones
Maintain visitor management system with escort requirements and access logging
Deploy CCTV surveillance with minimum 90-day retention at all entry/exit points and critical areas
Conduct periodic physical security assessments and penetration tests

maturity.basic: Basic card access with limited CCTV coverage and informal visitor management
maturity.mature: Multi-factor access with zoned security, comprehensive CCTV with 90-day retention, formal visitor management, and regular physical security assessments
maturity.advanced: AI-powered surveillance analytics, mantrap entries, tailgating detection, real-time access anomaly alerting, and integration with logical access management

physical-security.name: Environmental Controls
physical-security.description: Data centre environmental monitoring and protection including temperature/humidity control, water leak detection, and fire suppression systems.
keyActivities: Maintain precision cooling with redundant HVAC systems and temperature/humidity monitoring
Deploy water leak detection sensors beneath raised floors and near cooling equipment
Install and maintain gas-based fire suppression systems suitable for IT equipment
Implement 24/7 environmental monitoring with automated alerting and escalation

maturity.basic: Basic HVAC and fire suppression with manual monitoring and limited alerting
maturity.mature: Redundant cooling, comprehensive environmental sensors, gas-based fire suppression, 24/7 automated monitoring, and annual system testing
maturity.advanced: Hot/cold aisle containment, predictive cooling analytics, integrated building management system (BMS), and automated shutdown procedures for environmental emergencies

physical-security.name: Power & Redundancy
physical-security.description: Redundant power supply infrastructure including UPS, generator backup, dual power feeds, and automatic transfer switching ensuring continuous data centre operations.
keyActivities: Maintain UPS systems sized for minimum 15-minute runtime to cover generator start-up
Deploy backup generators with automatic transfer switches and minimum 48-hour fuel autonomy
Implement dual power feeds from independent utility sources where available
Conduct quarterly power failover tests and annual generator load bank testing

maturity.basic: Single UPS and generator with manual transfer switching and infrequent testing
maturity.mature: N+1 redundant UPS, automatic transfer switches, dual utility feeds where available, and regular failover testing with documented results
maturity.advanced: 2N power redundancy, concurrent maintainability, real-time power monitoring and analytics, and automated load balancing across power paths

service-continuity.name: Service Level Management
service-continuity.description: Establish and maintain a comprehensive IT service level management framework that defines, monitors, and reports on service performance metrics aligned with business expectations and BNM regulatory requirements.
keyActivities: Define SLAs for all critical IT services including core banking, internet banking, mobile banking, RENTAS, FPX, and DuitNow connectivity with measurable KPIs
Establish service level monitoring dashboards with real-time tracking of availability, response time, throughput, and error rates against agreed thresholds
Conduct periodic SLA review meetings with business stakeholders and technology teams to assess performance trends and address breaches
Implement escalation procedures and penalty mechanisms for persistent SLA non-compliance by internal teams or external service providers
Maintain a service catalogue that maps IT services to business functions with clearly documented ownership, support hours, and recovery commitments

maturity.basic: SLAs are defined for critical systems such as core banking and payment channels but monitoring is largely manual with periodic reporting to management.
maturity.mature: Automated SLA monitoring is in place across all IT services with real-time dashboards, proactive alerting on threshold breaches, and structured monthly review processes with business units.
maturity.advanced: Predictive analytics drive SLA management with AI-based forecasting of potential breaches, continuous service optimization, and SLA metrics integrated into enterprise risk appetite reporting to BNM.

service-continuity.name: IT Service Continuity
service-continuity.description: Develop and maintain IT service continuity plans that ensure critical banking technology services can be sustained or rapidly restored during disruptive events, meeting BNM expectations for financial system resilience.
keyActivities: Develop IT service continuity plans for all critical banking systems including core banking, payment gateways, ATM switching, SWIFT, and regulatory reporting platforms
Define continuity strategies for each critical service including active-active, active-passive, and warm standby configurations based on business criticality and RTO requirements
Maintain up-to-date continuity documentation including system dependencies, recovery procedures, contact lists, and vendor escalation paths
Conduct tabletop exercises and simulation drills at least semi-annually to validate continuity plans and identify gaps in recovery procedures
Integrate IT service continuity plans with the institution's overall Business Continuity Management framework and BNM crisis management expectations

maturity.basic: IT service continuity plans exist for core banking and key payment systems but are document-based with limited testing beyond annual walkthroughs.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive continuity plans cover all critical IT services with regular simulation testing, automated failover for key systems, and integration with enterprise BCM processes.
maturity.advanced: Resilience-by-design architecture ensures continuous availability through multi-site active-active deployments, chaos engineering practices to proactively identify failure modes, and real-time continuity posture dashboards reported to the Board.

service-continuity.name: Disaster Recovery
service-continuity.description: Establish and maintain disaster recovery capabilities for critical banking technology infrastructure ensuring that recovery time and recovery point objectives are met, with regular testing validated against BNM operational resilience expectations.
keyActivities: Define RTO and RPO for all critical systems with Board-approved targets aligned to BNM requirements — typically RTO of 4 hours or less for Tier-1 payment and core banking systems
Maintain a disaster recovery site with adequate infrastructure capacity, network connectivity, and data replication to support full recovery of critical banking operations
Conduct full-scale DR drills at least annually with actual failover to the DR site, validating end-to-end recovery of core banking, RENTAS, FPX, DuitNow, and internet/mobile banking services
Document and track DR test results, gaps identified, and remediation actions with reporting to the Board Risk Committee and retention for BNM examination
Ensure DR plans address data centre scenarios including total site loss, partial infrastructure failure, and cyber-attack-induced recovery requiring clean environment rebuild

maturity.basic: A DR site exists with basic replication for core banking and payment systems; DR drills are conducted annually with documented results but recovery times often exceed stated RTOs.
maturity.mature: Near-real-time replication is in place for all critical systems with semi-annual DR drills achieving target RTOs; automated failover is implemented for key payment infrastructure.
maturity.advanced: Zero or near-zero RPO achieved through synchronous replication across geographically separated sites, with quarterly DR exercises including cyber recovery scenarios and automated orchestration that consistently meets sub-2-hour RTO for all Tier-1 systems.

service-continuity.name: Business Impact Analysis
service-continuity.description: Conduct and maintain business impact analyses for technology services to determine criticality, interdependencies, and acceptable downtime thresholds that inform continuity and disaster recovery planning for Malaysian banking operations.
keyActivities: Perform BIA for all technology services mapping them to critical banking business functions including retail banking, treasury, payments (RENTAS, FPX, DuitNow), trade finance, and regulatory reporting
Identify and document upstream and downstream system dependencies including third-party services, shared infrastructure, and inter-bank connectivity
Determine Maximum Tolerable Downtime, RTO, and RPO for each critical technology service based on financial impact, regulatory obligations, and customer impact assessments
Review and update BIA at least annually or upon significant changes to technology infrastructure, business processes, or regulatory requirements from BNM

maturity.basic: BIA is conducted for major systems such as core banking and payment channels with basic impact categorisation, reviewed annually as part of BCM planning.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive BIA covers all technology services with quantified financial and operational impact metrics, documented interdependency maps, and formal linkage to DR and continuity planning.
maturity.advanced: Dynamic BIA models are maintained with real-time dependency mapping, automated impact recalculation when infrastructure changes occur, and scenario-based analysis that informs strategic resilience investments presented to the Board.

service-continuity.name: Crisis Communication
service-continuity.description: Establish crisis communication plans and protocols for technology and cyber incidents ensuring timely, accurate, and coordinated communication with internal stakeholders, BNM, law enforcement, customers, and the public as required under Malaysian regulatory expectations.
keyActivities: Develop and maintain a crisis communication plan that defines roles, responsibilities, communication channels, and escalation paths for technology and cybersecurity incidents
Establish notification procedures for BNM including timelines for initial notification, interim updates, and final incident reports in accordance with regulatory incident reporting requirements
Define customer communication templates and channels for service disruptions affecting internet banking, mobile banking, ATM networks, and payment services

Conduct crisis communication drills at least annually as part of broader DR and incident response exercises to test coordination between IT, risk, legal, compliance, corporate communications, and senior management
Maintain pre-approved communication templates for common scenarios including data breaches, prolonged service outages, cyber attacks, and fraud incidents to enable rapid response

maturity.basic: Crisis communication procedures exist with defined escalation paths to senior management and BNM but are largely manual with limited pre-prepared templates for common scenarios.
maturity.mature: Structured crisis communication framework with pre-approved templates, defined SLAs for stakeholder notification, integrated communication tools, and regular drills involving cross-functional teams.
maturity.advanced: Automated crisis communication orchestration with real-time stakeholder notification, AI-assisted situation assessment for communication prioritisation, integrated media monitoring, and post-crisis sentiment analysis to continuously improve response effectiveness.

network-security.name: Network Architecture and Segmentation
network-security.description: Design and maintain a secure network architecture with appropriate segmentation to isolate critical banking systems, payment networks, and sensitive data zones, reducing lateral movement risk and containing potential breaches.
keyActivities: Implement network segmentation to isolate critical zones including core banking, payment processing (RENTAS, FPX, DuitNow, SWIFT), internet-facing services, and internal corporate networks
Maintain up-to-date network architecture diagrams documenting all zones, trust boundaries, traffic flows, and interconnections with third parties and inter-bank networks
Deploy micro-segmentation for high-value assets and sensitive data repositories to limit blast radius in the event of a compromise
Enforce strict inter-zone access controls using next-generation firewalls with application-layer filtering and deny-by-default policies between security zones
Review network architecture at least annually and after significant infrastructure changes to ensure alignment with defence-in-depth principles and BNM expectations

maturity.basic: Network segmentation separates core banking and payment zones from the corporate network using VLANs and firewalls with basic access control lists governing inter-zone traffic.
maturity.mature: Multi-layered segmentation with DMZs, dedicated payment network zones, and micro-segmentation for critical assets; software-defined networking enables dynamic policy enforcement with centralised visibility.
maturity.advanced: Zero-trust network architecture with identity-aware micro-segmentation, automated policy enforcement based on workload classification, continuous traffic analysis for anomaly detection, and real-time topology visualisation integrated with SOC operations.

network-security.name: Firewall Management
network-security.description: Implement and maintain a robust firewall management programme ensuring that all network perimeter and internal firewalls are configured, reviewed, and managed to protect critical Malaysian banking infrastructure from unauthorised access.
keyActivities: Establish and enforce a firewall rule change management process requiring risk assessment, business justification, security review, and approval before implementation
Conduct firewall rule reviews at least quarterly to identify and remove stale, overly permissive, or redundant rules, particularly for rules governing access to payment and core banking zones
Implement centralised firewall management with consistent policy enforcement, logging, and configuration backup across all firewall appliances
Deploy next-generation firewall capabilities including application-layer inspection, intrusion prevention, and TLS decryption for traffic entering and leaving sensitive zones

maturity.basic: Firewalls are deployed at network perimeters and between major zones with rule change processes in place; rule reviews are conducted semi-annually with basic documentation.
maturity.mature: Centralised firewall management platform with automated rule lifecycle tracking, quarterly rule recertification, compliance reporting, and integration with change management workflows.
maturity.advanced: Intent-based firewall policy management with automated rule optimisation, real-time compliance verification against security baselines, AI-driven anomaly detection in rule changes, and continuous assurance reporting to risk management.

network-security.name: Intrusion Detection and Prevention
network-security.description: Deploy and operate intrusion detection and prevention systems across critical network segments to detect, alert on, and block malicious network activity targeting Malaysian banking infrastructure and payment systems.
keyActivities: Deploy IDS/IPS sensors at critical network points including internet gateways, payment network interfaces (RENTAS, FPX, SWIFT), core banking perimeters, and inter-zone boundaries
Maintain up-to-date IDS/IPS signatures and detection rules with automated feeds supplemented by custom rules developed from threat intelligence relevant to the Malaysian financial sector
Tune detection rules to minimise false positives while maintaining detection efficacy, with regular review cycles aligned to the evolving threat landscape
Integrate IDS/IPS alerts with the Security Operations Centre SIEM platform for correlation, triage, and incident response workflow automation
Conduct periodic detection efficacy testing using network-based attack simulations and red team exercises to validate coverage against known TTPs targeting financial institutions

maturity.basic: IDS/IPS deployed at network perimeters with vendor-supplied signature sets; alerts are forwarded to the SOC for manual review and investigation.
maturity.mature: IDS/IPS coverage extends to internal segments and payment network zones with tuned rulesets, automated alert correlation in SIEM, and regular detection efficacy testing.
maturity.advanced: Network detection and response platform combines signature-based and ML-driven behavioural analysis with automated threat containment, full packet capture for forensics, and continuous validation through automated breach and attack simulation.

network-security.name: Wireless Security
network-security.description: Secure all wireless network infrastructure within banking premises ensuring that Wi-Fi networks cannot be exploited as an attack vector to access critical banking systems or sensitive customer data.
keyActivities: Implement WPA3-Enterprise or WPA2-Enterprise with 802.1X authentication and certificate-based validation for all corporate wireless networks within banking premises
Segregate wireless networks from the core banking and payment processing networks using dedicated VLANs and firewall controls with no direct routing to critical zones
Conduct periodic wireless security assessments including rogue access point detection, signal leakage analysis, and penetration testing of wireless infrastructure
Deploy a wireless intrusion prevention system to continuously monitor for unauthorised access points, evil twin attacks, and anomalous wireless activity within branch and headquarters premises

maturity.basic: Corporate wireless networks use WPA2-Enterprise authentication and are segregated from the core banking network; periodic manual scans for rogue access points are conducted.
maturity.mature: Centralised wireless management with automated rogue AP detection, NAC integration for device posture validation before network access, and regular wireless penetration testing.
maturity.advanced: AI-driven wireless intrusion prevention with real-time anomaly detection, automated containment of rogue devices, dynamic access policies based on user identity and device trust score, and continuous compliance monitoring across all banking premises.

network-security.name: Remote Access Security
network-security.description: Implement secure remote access controls for banking staff and authorised third parties, ensuring that remote connectivity to critical systems is authenticated, encrypted, monitored, and compliant with BNM technology risk management expectations.
keyActivities: Enforce multi-factor authentication for all remote access connections to the banking network, with additional authentication requirements for access to core banking and payment systems
Deploy enterprise VPN or zero-trust network access solutions with strong encryption, device posture validation, and split tunnelling restrictions for connections to sensitive environments
Implement privileged access management for remote administrative sessions to critical infrastructure with session recording, time-limited access, and just-in-time elevation
Monitor and log all remote access sessions with real-time alerting on anomalous patterns such as unusual login times, geographic anomalies, or concurrent sessions from multiple locations

maturity.basic: VPN-based remote access with MFA is deployed for all staff; remote access to production systems requires additional approval and is logged for audit purposes.
maturity.mature: Zero-trust network access with continuous device posture assessment, PAM controls for administrative remote sessions, and automated anomaly detection on remote access patterns.
maturity.advanced: Adaptive remote access with real-time risk scoring that dynamically adjusts access permissions based on user behaviour, device health, threat intelligence, and contextual factors, with full session analytics and automated response to high-risk sessions.

network-security.name: DNS Security
network-security.description: Implement DNS security controls to protect banking infrastructure from DNS-based attacks including cache poisoning, DNS tunnelling, and domain hijacking that could redirect customers or compromise critical transaction flows.
keyActivities: Deploy DNSSEC for all externally facing banking domains to prevent DNS spoofing and cache poisoning attacks against customer-facing services including internet banking and mobile banking portals
Implement DNS filtering and threat intelligence-based domain blocking to prevent communication with known malicious domains, command-and-control infrastructure, and phishing sites
Monitor DNS query logs for indicators of DNS tunnelling, data exfiltration, and domain generation algorithm activity using integration with the SOC SIEM platform
Maintain redundant DNS infrastructure with DDoS protection for critical banking domain resolution to ensure availability of internet-facing services

maturity.basic: Internal DNS servers are hardened and DNSSEC is implemented for primary banking domains; basic DNS logging is enabled and forwarded to the SIEM for monitoring.
maturity.mature: DNS security platform provides real-time threat intelligence-based filtering, DNS query analytics for anomaly detection, and automated blocking of newly observed malicious domains.
maturity.advanced: Advanced DNS security with encrypted DNS protocols, machine learning-based detection of DNS tunnelling and DGA domains, automated threat response integration with SOC playbooks, and comprehensive DNS traffic analytics for threat hunting.

network-security.name: Network Monitoring
network-security.description: Establish comprehensive network monitoring capabilities to maintain visibility over all network traffic, detect anomalous behaviour, and support rapid incident detection and response across the banking technology environment.
keyActivities: Deploy network monitoring tools covering all critical segments including core banking, payment processing, internet-facing DMZ, inter-bank connectivity, and branch WAN links
Implement network traffic analysis with baseline profiling to detect anomalous traffic patterns, unusual data transfers, and lateral movement indicative of compromise
Maintain real-time network performance monitoring with alerting on degradation, capacity thresholds, and availability issues for critical banking services
Integrate network monitoring data with the SOC SIEM platform for correlation with security events, endpoint telemetry, and threat intelligence feeds
Retain network flow data and full packet captures for critical segments in accordance with BNM record retention requirements to support forensic investigation

maturity.basic: Network monitoring covers core infrastructure with SNMP-based availability and performance monitoring; NetFlow data is collected for major network segments and reviewed during incident investigations.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive network monitoring with traffic analysis, automated anomaly detection, and integration with SIEM for correlated security alerting across all banking network segments.
maturity.advanced: Network detection and response platform with ML-driven traffic analysis, encrypted traffic inspection, automated threat containment based on network indicators, and real-time network forensics capability integrated with the SOC workflow.

backup-and-recovery.name: Backup Management
backup-and-recovery.description: Implement and maintain a comprehensive data backup programme for all critical banking systems ensuring that backup data is complete, recoverable, securely stored, and aligned with defined RPO requirements and BNM expectations for data protection.
keyActivities: Define and implement backup schedules for all critical systems including core banking databases, payment transaction logs, customer records, and regulatory reporting data aligned with RPO requirements
Store backup copies in geographically separated locations with encryption at rest and in transit, ensuring backup media is protected to the same classification level as the source data
Conduct regular backup restoration testing at least quarterly for critical systems to validate backup integrity, completeness, and the ability to meet stated recovery time objectives
Implement immutable backup copies for critical data to protect against ransomware encryption and ensure recoverability even in scenarios where primary and secondary storage are compromised
Monitor backup job completion, success rates, and storage capacity with automated alerting on failures and trending analysis to proactively address backup infrastructure issues

maturity.basic: Scheduled backups run daily for critical banking systems with offsite tape or disk-based copies; backup restoration is tested annually with documented results.
maturity.mature: Automated backup management with continuous protection for critical databases, immutable backup copies, quarterly restoration testing across all tiers, and integrated monitoring with automated failure remediation.
maturity.advanced: Intelligent backup orchestration with application-aware continuous data protection, instant recovery capabilities, automated compliance verification against RPO targets, air-gapped immutable copies with cyber vault isolation, and backup analytics driving capacity planning.

third-party-management.name: Vendor Risk Assessment
third-party-management.description: Conduct thorough risk assessments of all technology vendors and service providers before engagement and on an ongoing basis, ensuring that third-party risks to critical banking operations are identified, evaluated, and mitigated in line with BNM outsourcing and third-party risk requirements.
keyActivities: Perform pre-engagement due diligence assessments covering information security posture, financial stability, regulatory compliance, business continuity capabilities, and data handling practices of prospective technology vendors
Classify vendors by criticality based on their access to sensitive data, connectivity to banking systems, and impact on critical operations including payment processing and customer-facing services
Conduct annual reassessment of critical and high-risk vendors including review of SOC 2 Type II or equivalent audit reports, penetration test results, and incident history
Maintain a centralised vendor risk register with risk ratings, assessment findings, remediation tracking, and contractual compliance status for all technology service providers
Ensure vendor risk assessments evaluate data residency and cross-border data transfer implications in compliance with PDPA and BNM requirements for Malaysian banking data

maturity.basic: Vendor risk assessments are conducted for critical technology providers using standardised questionnaires prior to engagement with annual reviews based on self-attestation.
maturity.mature: Tiered vendor risk assessment programme with differentiated assessment rigour based on vendor criticality, continuous monitoring of critical vendors, and integration with enterprise risk management processes.
maturity.advanced: Automated continuous vendor risk monitoring combining real-time threat intelligence, financial health indicators, regulatory action tracking, and security rating services, with dynamic risk scoring that triggers reassessment workflows when vendor risk posture deteriorates.

third-party-management.name: Outsourcing Governance
third-party-management.description: Establish a governance framework for technology outsourcing arrangements that ensures the institution retains effective oversight, control, and accountability over outsourced functions in compliance with BNM outsourcing policy requirements.
keyActivities: Maintain an outsourcing policy approved by the Board that defines the governance framework, roles and responsibilities, risk appetite, and approval authorities for technology outsourcing arrangements

Ensure outsourcing contracts include mandatory provisions for audit rights, data protection, business continuity, incident notification, subcontracting controls, and termination assistance in accordance with BNM requirements
Establish an outsourcing oversight committee with representation from technology, risk, compliance, and business units to govern material outsourcing arrangements and monitor performance
Notify BNM of material outsourcing arrangements as required and maintain documentation demonstrating that the institution retains the ability to manage and control outsourced activities

maturity.basic: An outsourcing policy is in place with Board approval; material outsourcing arrangements are documented with standard contractual provisions and reported to BNM as required.
maturity.mature: Structured outsourcing governance with a dedicated oversight committee, standardised contract templates incorporating all BNM-required provisions, regular performance reviews, and a centralised outsourcing register.
maturity.advanced: Enterprise-wide outsourcing governance platform with automated contract compliance monitoring, real-time vendor performance dashboards, integrated risk and audit workflows, and predictive analytics to identify emerging concentration risks across the outsourcing portfolio.

third-party-management.name: SLA Monitoring
third-party-management.description: Monitor and enforce service level agreements with technology vendors and service providers to ensure contracted performance levels are met and deficiencies are promptly addressed to protect critical banking operations.
keyActivities: Define measurable SLA metrics for all critical vendor services including availability, response times, resolution times, and security incident notification timelines
Implement automated SLA monitoring and reporting for critical vendor services with dashboards tracking performance against contracted thresholds
Conduct formal periodic vendor performance reviews at least quarterly for critical vendors and semi-annually for material vendors, documenting findings and agreed remediation actions
Enforce contractual remedies including service credits, escalation mechanisms, and where necessary termination provisions when persistent SLA breaches impact banking operations

maturity.basic: SLA metrics are defined in vendor contracts for critical services; performance is tracked through manual reporting with periodic review meetings to address breaches.
maturity.mature: Automated SLA monitoring dashboards for all critical vendor services with real-time breach alerting, structured quarterly performance reviews, and formal remediation tracking processes.
maturity.advanced: Predictive SLA management with trend analysis that forecasts potential breaches before they occur, automated escalation workflows, vendor performance benchmarking across the portfolio, and SLA analytics integrated into vendor risk scoring.

third-party-management.name: Vendor Exit Strategy
third-party-management.description: Develop and maintain exit strategies for critical technology vendor relationships to ensure that banking operations can be transitioned or brought in-house without unacceptable disruption, data loss, or service degradation.
keyActivities: Develop documented exit plans for all critical and material vendor relationships covering transition timelines, data migration procedures, knowledge transfer requirements, and alternative provider identification
Include contractual provisions for vendor exit assistance including data return or destruction obligations, transition support periods, and continued service provision during migration

Conduct periodic exit plan viability assessments to validate that transition scenarios are realistic, resource requirements are understood, and alternative service options remain available in the Malaysian market
Ensure exit plans address data sovereignty requirements under PDPA and BNM guidelines including secure data repatriation and certified data destruction by the outgoing vendor

maturity.basic: Exit clauses are included in critical vendor contracts with basic transition provisions; exit plans are documented at a high level but have not been tested.
maturity.mature: Detailed exit plans exist for all critical vendors with defined transition procedures, resource estimates, and alternative provider shortlists; plans are reviewed annually and validated through tabletop exercises.
maturity.advanced: Comprehensive vendor exit readiness programme with regular transition simulation exercises, maintained parallel capabilities for critical functions, automated data portability validation, and Board-level reporting on vendor dependency and exit readiness posture.

cloud-and-emerging-technology.name: Cloud Risk Assessment
cloud-and-emerging-technology.description: Conduct comprehensive risk assessments prior to adopting cloud services and on an ongoing basis, evaluating security, data sovereignty, regulatory compliance, and operational risks specific to Malaysian banking requirements and BNM cloud technology expectations.
keyActivities: Perform pre-adoption cloud risk assessments covering data classification, regulatory compliance with BNM and PDPA requirements, security architecture, data residency, vendor lock-in risks, and concentration risk

Evaluate cloud service provider security posture through review of SOC 2 Type II reports, ISO 27001 certification, CSA STAR assessment, and penetration testing results relevant to the services being consumed
Assess data sovereignty implications including data storage locations, cross-border data flows, and the ability to meet BNM requirements for maintaining certain banking data within Malaysia
Conduct annual reassessment of cloud service risks incorporating changes in provider security posture, regulatory requirements, threat landscape, and the institution's cloud usage profile

maturity.basic: Cloud risk assessments are conducted prior to adopting new cloud services using a standardised questionnaire covering security, compliance, and data residency requirements.
maturity.mature: Structured cloud risk assessment framework with tiered assessment rigour based on data sensitivity and service criticality, integration with cloud security posture management tools, and annual reassessment cycles.
maturity.advanced: Continuous cloud risk assessment with real-time monitoring of provider security posture through automated scanning, dynamic risk scoring based on actual usage patterns and data exposure, and integrated reporting to enterprise risk management with automated BNM compliance validation.

cloud-and-emerging-technology.name: Cloud Security Controls
cloud-and-emerging-technology.description: Implement and maintain technical security controls for cloud environments ensuring that banking data and workloads hosted in cloud platforms are protected to the same or higher standard as on-premises systems in compliance with BNM expectations.
keyActivities: Deploy cloud security posture management tools to continuously assess cloud configurations against CIS benchmarks and banking-specific security baselines across all cloud tenants
Implement cloud workload protection including runtime security, vulnerability management, and container security for banking applications deployed in cloud environments
Enforce identity and access management controls including MFA, least privilege, just-in-time access, and privileged session management for all cloud administrative access
Implement cloud-native encryption for data at rest and in transit with customer-managed encryption keys stored in dedicated HSM or key management services under the institution's control
Deploy cloud-native logging and monitoring with integration to the on-premises SOC SIEM for unified security monitoring across hybrid banking infrastructure

maturity.basic: Cloud security controls follow provider best practices with MFA for administrative access, encryption enabled for data at rest and in transit, and basic cloud logging forwarded to the SIEM.
maturity.mature: Cloud security posture management continuously validates configurations, CWPP protects workloads, customer-managed keys are used for sensitive data, and cloud security is fully integrated into SOC monitoring operations.
maturity.advanced: Unified cloud-native application protection platform combining CSPM, CWPP, CIEM, and DSPM with automated remediation, shift-left security integration in CI/CD pipelines, and AI-driven cloud threat detection with automated response playbooks.

cloud-and-emerging-technology.name: Cloud Data Residency
cloud-and-emerging-technology.description: Establish and enforce data residency policies for cloud-hosted banking data ensuring compliance with BNM requirements, PDPA obligations, and institutional data sovereignty commitments regarding the storage and processing of Malaysian banking data.
keyActivities: Define data residency policies specifying which categories of banking data must remain within Malaysia and which may be stored or processed in approved offshore locations subject to risk assessment and regulatory approval
Implement technical controls to enforce data residency requirements including cloud region restrictions, data loss prevention policies, and geo-fencing of data storage and processing
Maintain a data residency register documenting where all categories of banking data are stored and processed across cloud services, mapped to regulatory requirements and data classification

Monitor and audit compliance with data residency policies including periodic verification of data storage locations and cross-border data transfer activities with reporting to compliance and the Board

maturity.basic: Data residency policy requires critical banking data to remain in Malaysia; cloud services are configured to use Malaysian or approved regional data centres with periodic manual verification.
maturity.mature: Technical controls enforce data residency with cloud region restrictions and DLP policies; a comprehensive data residency register is maintained with automated compliance monitoring and regular reporting to BNM.
maturity.advanced: Real-time data residency assurance through automated data flow mapping, continuous compliance verification using cloud-native policy engines, instant alerting on residency violations, and integrated reporting across multi-cloud environments with regulatory audit trail.

cloud-and-emerging-technology.name: Cloud Portability and Exit
cloud-and-emerging-technology.description: Ensure cloud deployments are designed for portability and that comprehensive exit strategies exist to migrate workloads and data away from cloud service providers without unacceptable disruption to banking operations.
keyActivities: Design cloud deployments using portable architectures including containerisation, infrastructure-as-code, and abstraction layers to minimise vendor lock-in for critical banking applications
Develop documented cloud exit plans for each critical cloud service covering data extraction procedures, application migration steps, timeline estimates, and alternative deployment targets
Include contractual provisions for cloud exit assistance, data return in standard formats, transition support periods, and certified data destruction upon completion of migration
Conduct periodic cloud exit plan validation through tabletop exercises or partial migration drills to ensure that exit procedures are viable and resource requirements are understood

maturity.basic: Cloud exit clauses are included in contracts for critical cloud services; basic exit plans document data extraction procedures but have not been tested practically.
maturity.mature: Cloud deployments use containerised and portable architectures where feasible; detailed exit plans exist with defined procedures, resource requirements, and alternative targets, validated through annual tabletop exercises.
maturity.advanced: Cloud-native portability architecture with multi-cloud deployment capabilities, automated workload migration tooling, regular exit drills with actual data migration testing, and Board-level reporting on cloud dependency and exit readiness across the entire cloud portfolio.

cloud-and-emerging-technology.name: AI and ML Governance
cloud-and-emerging-technology.description: Establish governance frameworks for the responsible adoption and use of artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies in banking operations, addressing model risk, fairness, transparency, and accountability in line with BNM expectations for emerging technology governance.
keyActivities: Develop an AI and ML governance policy approved by the Board defining acceptable use cases, risk appetite, ethical principles, and governance responsibilities for AI/ML deployments in banking operations

Implement a model risk management framework covering model development, validation, deployment, monitoring, and decommissioning with independent validation for models used in credit decisioning, fraud detection, and AML
Establish fairness and bias monitoring for AI/ML models used in customer-facing decisions ensuring compliance with BNM fair treatment expectations and non-discrimination requirements
Maintain transparency and explainability standards for AI/ML models with documentation of model logic, training data provenance, performance metrics, and known limitations
Implement human oversight controls including escalation mechanisms, override capabilities, and periodic human review of AI/ML-driven decisions affecting customers

maturity.basic: AI/ML governance policy is in place with Board awareness; model inventory is maintained for critical AI/ML models with basic validation and documentation requirements.
maturity.mature: Structured model risk management framework with independent model validation, bias testing, ongoing performance monitoring, and formal governance committee oversight of AI/ML deployments across the institution.
maturity.advanced: Enterprise AI governance platform with automated model monitoring for drift and bias, real-time explainability dashboards, federated model risk management, regulatory-aligned audit trails, and proactive engagement with BNM on evolving AI governance expectations.

cloud-and-emerging-technology.name: Emerging Technology Risk Assessment
cloud-and-emerging-technology.description: Establish a structured process for evaluating risks associated with adopting emerging technologies such as blockchain, quantum computing, IoT, and decentralised finance, ensuring informed decision-making aligned with the institution's risk appetite and BNM technology risk expectations.
keyActivities: Develop a technology innovation risk assessment framework that evaluates security, regulatory, operational, and reputational risks before adopting new or emerging technologies for banking use cases

Conduct horizon scanning to identify emerging technology trends relevant to Malaysian banking including DeFi, central bank digital currencies, quantum computing threats to cryptography, and IoT in branch operations
Perform proof-of-concept evaluations in isolated sandbox environments before approving emerging technology deployments in production banking infrastructure
Engage with BNM, industry bodies such as the Association of Banks in Malaysia, and peer institutions to understand regulatory expectations and industry best practices for emerging technology adoption

maturity.basic: Emerging technology proposals are evaluated through the existing risk assessment process with security and compliance review before any deployment to the production environment.
maturity.mature: Dedicated innovation risk assessment framework with structured evaluation criteria, sandbox environments for proof-of-concept testing, and formal governance approval process involving technology, risk, and compliance stakeholders.
maturity.advanced: Strategic technology risk intelligence programme with systematic horizon scanning, pre-emptive risk assessment of emerging technologies before industry adoption, active engagement in BNM regulatory sandboxes, and Board-level technology strategy reporting that integrates emerging risk analysis with innovation roadmap planning.

access-control.name: Access Control Framework
access-control.description: Establish a comprehensive access control policy governing logical and physical access to all technology assets, aligned with BNM RMiT least-privilege and need-to-know principles.
keyActivities: Define and document an enterprise access control policy covering all technology environments
Classify information assets and map access rights to job roles and responsibilities
Enforce segregation of duties across critical banking functions such as treasury, payments, and core banking
Conduct annual policy reviews incorporating regulatory changes from BNM and industry best practices

maturity.basic: Documented access control policy exists with basic role definitions and annual review cycle.
maturity.mature: Automated role-based access provisioning integrated with HR lifecycle events and periodic attestation workflows.
maturity.advanced: Attribute-based access control (ABAC) dynamically enforces context-aware policies with continuous compliance monitoring and analytics.

access-control.name: Identity and Access Management
access-control.description: Implement centralised identity and access management controls to authenticate, authorise, and manage user identities across all banking systems and digital channels.
keyActivities: Deploy a centralised IAM platform with directory integration for all enterprise and customer-facing systems
Automate user provisioning and de-provisioning tied to joiner-mover-leaver processes
Implement unique user identification with no shared or generic accounts for production systems
Maintain comprehensive audit trails of all access grants, modifications, and revocations

maturity.basic: Centralised directory services with manual provisioning workflows and documented account management procedures.
maturity.mature: Automated identity lifecycle management with self-service access requests, approval workflows, and integration with core banking HR systems.
maturity.advanced: Identity governance with AI-driven access recommendations, anomaly detection on entitlements, and real-time identity analytics across hybrid environments.

access-control.name: Multi-Factor Authentication
access-control.description: Enforce multi-factor authentication for all privileged access, remote access, and customer-facing digital banking channels as mandated by BNM RMiT.
keyActivities: Implement MFA for all administrative, privileged, and remote access to technology infrastructure
Deploy strong customer authentication for internet banking, mobile banking, and high-risk transactions
Evaluate and adopt phishing-resistant MFA methods such as FIDO2 hardware tokens for critical systems
Monitor MFA adoption rates and enforce compliance with fallback controls for exceptions

maturity.basic: MFA deployed for privileged and remote access using OTP-based methods with documented exception management.
maturity.mature: Risk-based adaptive MFA across all access channels with push notification and biometric options for digital banking services.
maturity.advanced: Passwordless and phishing-resistant authentication (FIDO2/WebAuthn) as the default, with continuous authentication signals and behavioural biometrics.

access-control.name: Password Management
access-control.description: Establish and enforce password policies across all systems that meet BNM RMiT complexity, rotation, and storage requirements for financial institution environments.
keyActivities: Define and enforce password complexity, length, and expiration policies across all enterprise systems
Implement technical controls to prevent password reuse, dictionary words, and compromised credential usage
Deploy enterprise password vaulting solutions for service accounts and shared credentials
Conduct periodic audits of password policy compliance across all platforms including legacy core banking systems

maturity.basic: Password policies enforced via Active Directory GPO with minimum complexity requirements and 90-day rotation.
maturity.mature: Enterprise password manager deployed with breached-password screening, service account vaulting, and automated rotation for non-human identities.
maturity.advanced: Transition towards passwordless authentication with password policies serving as a compensating layer, supplemented by credential intelligence feeds and real-time compromise detection.

access-control.name: Privileged Access Management
access-control.description: Implement controls to secure, monitor, and audit all privileged access to critical banking technology infrastructure, databases, and applications.
keyActivities: Deploy a PAM solution with session recording, credential vaulting, and just-in-time access for all privileged accounts
Implement break-glass procedures with dual-authorisation for emergency privileged access to production systems
Enforce privileged session isolation through jump servers or secure access workstations for critical environments
Review and certify all privileged entitlements quarterly with sign-off from system owners and IT risk

maturity.basic: Privileged accounts inventoried with password vaulting and basic session logging for critical systems.
maturity.mature: Full PAM platform with just-in-time provisioning, session recording, command filtering, and automated credential rotation across all tiers.
maturity.advanced: Zero standing privileges model with all privileged access granted ephemerally, behaviour analytics on privileged sessions, and automated anomaly response.

access-control.name: Access Review and Recertification
access-control.description: Conduct periodic reviews and recertification of all user access rights to ensure continued appropriateness and detect access creep or orphaned accounts across banking systems.
keyActivities: Perform quarterly access recertification for privileged accounts and semi-annual reviews for standard user access
Automate detection of orphaned accounts, dormant accounts, and segregation-of-duty violations
Require business owner attestation for all access entitlements with documented evidence of review decisions
Track and remediate access review findings within defined SLAs with escalation to IT risk for overdue items

maturity.basic: Periodic manual access reviews conducted via spreadsheets with evidence retained for audit purposes.
maturity.mature: Automated access certification campaigns with workflow-driven approvals, violation detection, and integration with IAM provisioning for revocation.
maturity.advanced: Continuous access intelligence with machine-learning-driven risk scoring of entitlements, peer-group analysis, and automated micro-certifications triggered by role or system changes.

cybersecurity.name: Incident Response Plan
cybersecurity.description: Establish and maintain a cyber incident response plan covering detection, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident review, meeting BNM notification timelines.
keyActivities: Develop and maintain a cyber incident response plan with clearly defined roles, escalation paths, and communication protocols
Conduct tabletop exercises and simulated incident drills at least annually with involvement from senior management
Define incident severity classification aligned with BNM RMiT notification thresholds and reporting timelines
Perform post-incident reviews with root cause analysis and track remediation actions to closure
Maintain an incident response playbook library covering ransomware, data breach, DDoS, and insider threat scenarios

maturity.basic: Documented incident response plan with defined roles and annual tabletop exercises covering key scenarios.
maturity.mature: Integrated incident response with automated orchestration, predefined playbooks, and regular purple-team exercises validating detection and response capabilities.
maturity.advanced: SOAR-driven automated response with adaptive playbooks, threat-intelligence-enriched triage, and continuous improvement through attack simulation and lessons-learned integration.

cybersecurity.name: Cybersecurity Strategy
cybersecurity.description: Define a board-approved cybersecurity strategy that articulates the institution's cyber risk appetite, strategic objectives, and resource allocation for cyber resilience.
keyActivities: Develop a multi-year cybersecurity strategy aligned with the institution's business strategy and BNM RMiT requirements
Obtain board approval for the cybersecurity strategy with annual progress reporting to the board risk committee
Define measurable cybersecurity objectives, key risk indicators, and investment priorities
Benchmark cybersecurity maturity against industry frameworks and peer institutions in the Malaysian financial sector

maturity.basic: Board-approved cybersecurity strategy document with defined objectives and annual review cycle.
maturity.mature: Strategy linked to quantified cyber risk metrics with dedicated budget allocation, roadmap tracking, and quarterly board reporting on maturity progression.
maturity.advanced: Dynamic strategy with continuous risk quantification (e.g., FAIR), scenario-based investment modelling, and real-time alignment with evolving threat landscape and regulatory expectations.

cybersecurity.name: Cybersecurity Governance
cybersecurity.description: Establish cybersecurity governance structures with clear accountability, reporting lines, and oversight mechanisms from operational teams through to board level.
keyActivities: Appoint a CISO or equivalent with direct reporting to senior management and access to the board risk committee
