Establish a cybersecurity steering committee with cross-functional representation from business, risk, and technology
Define and communicate cybersecurity roles and responsibilities across three lines of defence
Implement cybersecurity performance metrics and dashboards for management and board reporting

maturity.basic: Designated CISO role with documented governance structure and periodic reporting to management.
maturity.mature: Formal cybersecurity committee with cross-functional membership, three-lines-of-defence model, and integrated risk reporting to board risk committee.
maturity.advanced: Cybersecurity governance embedded into enterprise risk management with real-time risk dashboards, automated compliance tracking, and proactive board engagement on emerging threats.

cybersecurity.name: Cyber Crisis Management
cybersecurity.description: Maintain a cyber crisis management framework that enables coordinated response to severe cyber events with clear escalation to senior management and BNM.
keyActivities: Develop a cyber crisis management plan with defined crisis thresholds, command structures, and decision authorities
Establish communication protocols for internal stakeholders, BNM, law enforcement (PDRM, CyberSecurity Malaysia), and affected customers
Conduct annual cyber crisis simulation exercises involving senior management and board members
Maintain pre-approved crisis communication templates and media response strategies
Define recovery time objectives for crisis scenarios and validate through simulation

maturity.basic: Documented cyber crisis management plan with defined escalation thresholds and annual simulation exercise.
maturity.mature: Integrated crisis management with cross-functional war room procedures, pre-staged communication templates, and coordinated exercises with industry peers and regulators.
maturity.advanced: Real-time crisis orchestration platform with automated severity assessment, dynamic stakeholder notification, and post-crisis analytics driving continuous improvement of response capabilities.

cybersecurity.name: SOC Operations
cybersecurity.description: Operate a Security Operations Centre providing continuous monitoring, detection, and initial response capabilities for cybersecurity events across all banking technology assets.
keyActivities: Establish 24x7 SOC operations with defined monitoring coverage, staffing levels, and skill requirements
Develop and maintain detection use cases and correlation rules aligned with MITRE ATT&CK and Malaysian banking threat landscape
Define SOC standard operating procedures for alert triage, investigation, escalation, and handover
Measure SOC effectiveness through mean-time-to-detect (MTTD), mean-time-to-respond (MTTR), and false-positive rates
Integrate SOC operations with incident response, threat intelligence, and vulnerability management functions

maturity.basic: SOC established with basic monitoring coverage, defined shift schedules, and documented triage procedures.
maturity.mature: Mature SOC with MITRE ATT&CK-mapped detection use cases, tiered analyst model, automated enrichment, and integrated threat intelligence feeds.
maturity.advanced: Next-generation SOC with AI-augmented detection, automated investigation workflows, proactive threat hunting, and continuous detection engineering with purple-team validation.

cybersecurity.name: SIEM Implementation
cybersecurity.description: Deploy and maintain a Security Information and Event Management platform to aggregate, correlate, and analyse security events from all critical banking technology systems.
keyActivities: Deploy SIEM with log ingestion from all critical systems including core banking, internet banking, network infrastructure, and security controls
Develop correlation rules and detection analytics tuned for Malaysian banking threat scenarios
Ensure log integrity, retention, and tamper-evidence meeting BNM and Malaysian regulatory requirements
Integrate SIEM with threat intelligence feeds, vulnerability data, and asset inventory for contextual alerting

maturity.basic: SIEM deployed with log collection from critical systems and baseline correlation rules for common attack patterns.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive SIEM coverage with behavioural analytics, custom detection rules for banking-specific threats, and automated alert enrichment with contextual data.
maturity.advanced: Cloud-native or hybrid SIEM with machine-learning-driven anomaly detection, entity behaviour analytics (UEBA), and automated response orchestration via SOAR integration.

cybersecurity.name: Endpoint Protection
cybersecurity.description: Deploy advanced endpoint detection and response capabilities across all endpoints including workstations, servers, and mobile devices to prevent, detect, and respond to malware and advanced threats.
keyActivities: Deploy EDR/XDR solutions across all managed endpoints with centralised management and policy enforcement
Implement application whitelisting on critical servers and high-risk endpoints such as treasury and payment systems
Maintain endpoint hardening baselines aligned with CIS benchmarks for all operating system types in use
Monitor endpoint telemetry for indicators of compromise and integrate alerts into SOC workflows

maturity.basic: Enterprise antivirus with centralised management, automatic signature updates, and basic endpoint hardening standards.
maturity.mature: EDR platform with behavioural detection, automated containment, and integration with SIEM for correlated threat detection across endpoints.
maturity.advanced: XDR with cross-telemetry correlation across endpoints, network, and cloud, AI-driven threat detection, automated response actions, and proactive threat hunting on endpoint data.

cybersecurity.name: Email Security
cybersecurity.description: Implement multi-layered email security controls to protect against phishing, business email compromise, malware delivery, and data exfiltration via email channels.
keyActivities: Deploy email gateway security with anti-phishing, anti-malware, sandboxing, and URL rewriting capabilities
Implement DMARC, DKIM, and SPF records for all institutional email domains to prevent spoofing
Configure data loss prevention rules on outbound email to detect sensitive banking data leakage
Enable advanced threat protection for attachment detonation and impersonation detection

maturity.basic: Email gateway with anti-spam, anti-malware filtering, and basic SPF/DKIM/DMARC configuration.
maturity.mature: Advanced email security with sandboxing, URL rewriting, impersonation protection, and automated phishing response with user-reported email workflows.
maturity.advanced: AI-powered email security with natural language analysis for BEC detection, automated incident response for confirmed phishing, and continuous simulation-driven policy tuning.

cybersecurity.name: Data Loss Prevention
cybersecurity.description: Implement data loss prevention controls to detect and prevent unauthorised exfiltration of sensitive banking data, customer PII, and regulated information across all channels.
keyActivities: Classify sensitive data types including customer PII, financial records, and card data per Malaysian PDPA and PCI DSS requirements
Deploy DLP controls across email, web, endpoint, and cloud storage channels with policy-based enforcement
Define DLP policies for critical data patterns such as NRIC numbers, bank account numbers, and credit card data
Monitor and investigate DLP policy violations with documented response procedures and escalation to compliance

maturity.basic: DLP policies deployed on email and web channels for known sensitive data patterns with basic alerting.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive DLP across all channels with content inspection, contextual analysis, automated blocking for high-severity violations, and integration with data classification.
maturity.advanced: Intelligent DLP with machine-learning-based content classification, user behaviour analytics for insider threat detection, and adaptive policies that adjust based on risk context.

cybersecurity.name: Vulnerability Assessment
cybersecurity.description: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments across all technology assets to identify, prioritise, and remediate security weaknesses before exploitation.
keyActivities: Perform monthly vulnerability scans across all network segments, servers, endpoints, and applications
Prioritise vulnerabilities using risk-based scoring that considers asset criticality, exploitability, and threat context
Define remediation SLAs based on vulnerability severity: critical within 14 days, high within 30 days, medium within 90 days
Track vulnerability remediation metrics and report trends to IT risk committee
Conduct vulnerability assessments prior to production deployment and after significant infrastructure changes

maturity.basic: Scheduled vulnerability scans with severity-based remediation SLAs and periodic reporting to management.
maturity.mature: Continuous vulnerability scanning with risk-based prioritisation, automated ticketing for remediation, and integration with patch management and change control.
maturity.advanced: Risk-based vulnerability management with threat-intelligence enrichment, attack-path analysis, predictive exploitability scoring, and automated remediation for defined vulnerability classes.

cybersecurity.name: Penetration Testing
cybersecurity.description: Conduct periodic penetration testing of critical systems, applications, and network infrastructure to validate the effectiveness of security controls in a Malaysian banking context.
keyActivities: Perform annual penetration testing of all internet-facing systems, critical applications, and internal network segments
Engage qualified independent testers with financial services experience for external penetration tests
Scope penetration tests to cover web applications, APIs, mobile banking apps, network infrastructure, and social engineering
Track and remediate all penetration testing findings with retesting to confirm effective closure

maturity.basic: Annual penetration testing by qualified external providers with finding remediation tracked to closure.
maturity.mature: Semi-annual penetration testing with scenario-based testing aligned to banking threat landscape, including social engineering and physical security assessments.
maturity.advanced: Continuous penetration testing programme with adversary simulation, objective-based testing aligned to crown-jewel assets, and integration with red-team and purple-team exercises.

cybersecurity.name: Red Team Exercises
cybersecurity.description: Conduct adversary simulation exercises using red-team methodologies to test the institution's detection and response capabilities against realistic advanced threat scenarios.
keyActivities: Plan and execute red-team exercises simulating advanced persistent threat actors targeting the Malaysian financial sector
Define realistic objectives such as accessing core banking data, manipulating payment systems, or exfiltrating customer records
Conduct purple-team debriefs to identify detection gaps and improve SOC detection capabilities
Document findings with tactical and strategic recommendations and track remediation through risk register

maturity.basic: Annual red-team exercise with defined scope and objectives, findings documented and tracked for remediation.
maturity.mature: Regular red-team exercises using threat-intelligence-informed scenarios with purple-team collaboration to improve detection coverage against MITRE ATT&CK techniques.
maturity.advanced: Continuous adversary emulation programme with automated breach-and-attack simulation, real-time detection gap analysis, and integration into security control validation lifecycle.

cybersecurity.name: Bug Bounty and Responsible Disclosure
cybersecurity.description: Establish a vulnerability disclosure programme or bug bounty initiative to leverage external security researchers in identifying vulnerabilities in customer-facing banking systems.
keyActivities: Define a responsible disclosure policy with clear scope, rules of engagement, and legal safe harbour provisions
Establish a vulnerability intake process with triage, validation, and remediation workflows
Consider a bug bounty programme for internet-facing banking applications and digital channels
Track disclosed vulnerabilities through remediation and provide timely feedback to researchers

maturity.basic: Published responsible disclosure policy with a dedicated security contact email and documented intake procedures.
maturity.mature: Managed bug bounty programme on an established platform with defined reward tiers, SLA-driven triage, and integration with vulnerability management.
maturity.advanced: Mature bug bounty programme with private researcher pools, continuous scope expansion, VIP programmes for top researchers, and analytics-driven programme optimisation.

cybersecurity.name: Threat Intelligence Program
cybersecurity.description: Establish a cyber threat intelligence programme that collects, analyses, and operationalises threat intelligence relevant to the Malaysian financial services sector.
keyActivities: Subscribe to threat intelligence feeds relevant to ASEAN and Malaysian financial sector threats including from MyCERT and FS-ISAC
Develop intelligence analysis capability to produce tactical, operational, and strategic threat assessments
Operationalise threat intelligence into detection rules, vulnerability prioritisation, and risk assessments
Participate in industry threat-sharing communities including BNM-facilitated and ASEAN financial sector groups
Produce periodic threat landscape briefings for senior management and board risk committee

maturity.basic: Subscription to commercial and government threat feeds with manual dissemination to SOC and IT security teams.
maturity.mature: Dedicated threat intelligence function producing contextualised intelligence products, automated feed integration into SIEM, and active participation in FS-ISAC and local sharing communities.
maturity.advanced: Intelligence-driven security operations with predictive analysis, custom threat modelling for institutional crown jewels, automated TIP integration, and strategic intelligence informing board-level risk decisions.

cybersecurity.name: Threat Hunting
cybersecurity.description: Conduct proactive threat hunting activities to identify advanced threats and adversary presence that may evade automated detection controls in banking environments.
keyActivities: Develop threat hunting hypotheses based on threat intelligence, MITRE ATT&CK techniques, and banking sector attack patterns
Conduct regular threat hunting campaigns across endpoint, network, and cloud telemetry data
Document hunting methodologies, findings, and convert successful hunts into automated detection rules
Train SOC analysts in threat hunting techniques and allocate dedicated hunting time within SOC operations

maturity.basic: Ad-hoc threat hunting conducted in response to threat advisories and intelligence reports using SIEM and EDR tools.
maturity.mature: Structured threat hunting programme with hypothesis-driven campaigns, dedicated hunters, and a feedback loop converting hunt findings into detection engineering.
maturity.advanced: Data-science-driven threat hunting with advanced analytics, custom tooling, automated hypothesis generation, and continuous hunting integrated into daily SOC operations.

cybersecurity.name: IOC Management
cybersecurity.description: Manage indicators of compromise through systematic collection, validation, distribution, and operationalisation across security monitoring infrastructure.
keyActivities: Establish a threat intelligence platform for IOC aggregation, deduplication, and lifecycle management
Automate IOC distribution to perimeter defences, SIEM, EDR, and email security controls
Validate and score IOCs for relevance and confidence before operationalisation to reduce false positives
Define IOC retention and expiry policies to maintain detection accuracy and platform performance

maturity.basic: Manual IOC management with feeds ingested into SIEM and periodic updates to blocking lists on security controls.
maturity.mature: Automated IOC lifecycle management via TIP with confidence scoring, automated distribution to detection and prevention controls, and feedback loops on detection hits.
maturity.advanced: Intelligent IOC management with machine-learning-based scoring, automated enrichment and pivoting, STIX/TAXII-based sharing with industry peers, and real-time correlation with internal telemetry.

cybersecurity.name: Cyber Situational Awareness
cybersecurity.description: Maintain continuous awareness of the cyber threat landscape relevant to Malaysian financial services through monitoring, analysis, and information sharing.
keyActivities: Monitor threat advisories from BNM, MyCERT, CyberSecurity Malaysia, and international financial sector CERTs
Maintain a cyber threat dashboard providing real-time visibility into external threat landscape and internal security posture
Produce regular situational awareness briefings for IT security, business stakeholders, and senior management
Assess emerging threats for potential impact on the institution's technology environment and digital banking services

maturity.basic: Monitoring of regulatory and industry threat advisories with periodic situational reports to IT security management.
maturity.mature: Integrated cyber situational awareness with real-time dashboards, automated advisory processing, and regular briefings to senior management linked to institutional risk context.
maturity.advanced: Predictive situational awareness combining external threat intelligence, internal telemetry, dark web monitoring, and geopolitical analysis to anticipate threats to the Malaysian financial sector.

digital-services.name: Digital Channel Security
digital-services.description: Implement security controls across all digital banking channels including internet banking, mobile banking, and open banking to protect customer transactions and data.
keyActivities: Enforce TLS 1.2 or higher with strong cipher suites across all customer-facing digital banking channels
Implement certificate pinning for mobile banking applications and validate certificate chains
Deploy web application firewalls with rulesets tuned for banking application attack patterns
Conduct security assessments of all digital channels prior to launch and after significant changes

maturity.basic: Baseline security controls on digital channels with TLS enforcement, WAF deployment, and periodic security assessments.
maturity.mature: Defence-in-depth across all digital channels with runtime application protection, bot management, API security gateways, and continuous security monitoring.
maturity.advanced: Adaptive digital channel security with real-time risk scoring, behavioural analytics for anomaly detection, automated threat response, and DevSecOps-integrated continuous security validation.

digital-services.name: Mobile Application Security
digital-services.description: Secure mobile banking applications through hardening, integrity protection, and runtime defence mechanisms to protect against reverse engineering, tampering, and overlay attacks.
keyActivities: Implement mobile application hardening including code obfuscation, anti-tampering, and jailbreak/root detection
Conduct mobile application security testing (SAST, DAST, IAST) aligned with OWASP MASVS before each release
Deploy runtime application self-protection (RASP) for mobile banking apps to detect and respond to runtime threats
Implement secure local storage for sensitive data with platform-appropriate encryption (Keychain/Keystore)

maturity.basic: Mobile app security testing before release with basic hardening and secure storage implementation.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive mobile security with RASP, device integrity verification, secure communication channels, and automated security testing in CI/CD pipelines.
maturity.advanced: Advanced mobile protection with behavioural biometrics, device fingerprinting, real-time threat intelligence integration, and continuous security posture assessment of the mobile app ecosystem.

digital-services.name: API Security
digital-services.description: Secure APIs used for digital banking services, open banking, and third-party integrations with authentication, authorisation, rate limiting, and input validation controls.
keyActivities: Implement API gateway with OAuth 2.0/OpenID Connect authentication, rate limiting, and request validation
Enforce API schema validation, input sanitisation, and output encoding to prevent injection attacks
Maintain an API inventory and classify APIs by risk level with appropriate security controls per classification
Monitor API usage patterns for anomalies and implement automated blocking of suspicious API consumers
Conduct API-specific penetration testing covering OWASP API Security Top 10 risks

maturity.basic: API gateway deployed with authentication, basic rate limiting, and input validation for all exposed APIs.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive API security with schema enforcement, automated threat detection, API-specific WAF rules, and developer security guidelines integrated into API design process.
maturity.advanced: AI-driven API security with behavioural analysis of API consumers, automated vulnerability detection, API abuse prevention, and continuous compliance validation for open banking standards.

digital-services.name: Customer Authentication
digital-services.description: Implement strong customer authentication mechanisms for digital banking services that balance security with user experience while meeting BNM RMiT requirements.
keyActivities: Deploy multi-factor authentication for customer login to internet and mobile banking using secure methods
Implement risk-based authentication that escalates authentication strength based on transaction risk and context
Support secure authentication methods including biometrics, hardware tokens, and secure push notifications
Ensure authentication mechanisms are resistant to phishing, SIM-swap, and man-in-the-middle attacks

maturity.basic: MFA for customer digital banking access with SMS OTP and secure password policies.
maturity.mature: Risk-based adaptive authentication with biometric options, device binding, and step-up authentication for high-risk transactions such as fund transfers and payee registration.
maturity.advanced: Continuous customer authentication with behavioural biometrics, device trust scoring, and frictionless risk-based authentication reducing MFA prompts for trusted sessions while strengthening security for anomalous access.

digital-services.name: Transaction Verification
digital-services.description: Implement transaction signing and verification controls to ensure the integrity and authenticity of customer-initiated financial transactions through digital channels.
keyActivities: Implement transaction signing for high-risk transactions using out-of-band verification channels
Display transaction details (amount, recipient, account) in the verification channel to prevent transaction manipulation
Deploy dynamic linking between the authentication code and the specific transaction details being authorised
Monitor for transaction verification bypass attempts and implement anomaly detection on verification patterns

maturity.basic: OTP-based transaction verification for fund transfers and critical account changes with transaction details displayed.
maturity.mature: Dynamic transaction signing with secure push notifications displaying full transaction context, device binding, and resistance to real-time phishing and social engineering.
maturity.advanced: Cryptographic transaction signing with dynamic linking, hardware-backed verification on mobile devices, and real-time fraud analytics on transaction verification behaviour patterns.

digital-services.name: Session Management
digital-services.description: Implement secure session management controls for digital banking applications to prevent session hijacking, fixation, and unauthorised session reuse.
keyActivities: Enforce session timeout policies appropriate to channel risk: shorter timeouts for internet banking, configurable for mobile
Implement secure session token generation using cryptographically strong random number generators
Prevent concurrent sessions from multiple devices or locations with customer notification of active sessions
Invalidate sessions on password change, privilege escalation, and detected anomalies

maturity.basic: Session timeouts enforced with secure token generation and session invalidation on logout and password change.
maturity.mature: Advanced session management with device fingerprinting, concurrent session controls, anomaly-triggered session termination, and customer session visibility features.
maturity.advanced: Continuous session risk assessment with behavioural analysis, automatic re-authentication triggers, session binding to device and network context, and real-time session compromise detection.

digital-services.name: Fraud Detection
digital-services.description: Deploy real-time fraud detection and prevention capabilities across digital banking channels to identify and block fraudulent transactions and account takeover attempts.
keyActivities: Implement real-time transaction fraud monitoring with rules and machine-learning models for anomaly detection
Deploy device intelligence and behavioural analytics to detect account takeover and authorised push payment fraud
Define fraud detection rules covering transaction velocity, geo-anomaly, beneficiary risk, and amount thresholds
Establish fraud investigation workflows with integration to transaction blocking and customer notification capabilities
Report fraud trends and losses to management and participate in industry fraud intelligence sharing

maturity.basic: Rule-based fraud detection system monitoring digital banking transactions with manual investigation workflows.
maturity.mature: Hybrid fraud detection combining rules and machine-learning models, real-time transaction scoring, automated blocking for high-confidence fraud, and case management for investigations.
maturity.advanced: AI-driven fraud prevention with deep learning models, network analysis for mule account detection, real-time adaptive rules, and consortium-based fraud intelligence sharing across Malaysian banks.

digital-services.name: QR Code and E-Wallet Security
digital-services.description: Implement security controls for QR code-based payment systems and e-wallet services to prevent QR code manipulation, payment fraud, and unauthorised wallet access in line with BNM guidelines.
keyActivities: Validate QR code integrity and authenticity to prevent QR code substitution and injection attacks at merchant points
Implement transaction limits and velocity controls for QR-based and e-wallet payments aligned with BNM guidelines
Secure e-wallet provisioning, top-up, and withdrawal processes with appropriate authentication controls
Monitor QR payment and e-wallet transactions for fraud patterns including merchant impersonation and QR overlay attacks

maturity.basic: QR code validation controls with transaction limits and basic fraud monitoring for QR-based and e-wallet payments.
maturity.mature: Cryptographically signed QR codes, dynamic QR with expiry, merchant verification, and real-time fraud detection for DuitNow QR and e-wallet transactions.
maturity.advanced: Advanced QR security with device attestation, location-based risk analysis, AI-driven fraud prevention across payment channels, and real-time merchant risk scoring integrated with PayNet ecosystem.

assurance-and-compliance.name: IT Audit Program
assurance-and-compliance.description: Establish a risk-based IT audit programme that provides independent assurance on the effectiveness of technology risk management, cybersecurity controls, and IT governance.
keyActivities: Develop an annual risk-based IT audit plan covering critical technology domains, cybersecurity, and regulatory requirements
Execute IT audits assessing control design and operating effectiveness with reference to BNM RMiT requirements
Track audit findings and management action plans with escalation for overdue items to audit committee
Perform follow-up audits to validate remediation effectiveness and closure of identified deficiencies

maturity.basic: Annual IT audit plan with coverage of key technology risk areas and documented findings tracking.
maturity.mature: Risk-based IT audit programme with continuous auditing techniques, data analytics, and integrated findings management linked to enterprise risk register.
maturity.advanced: Agile IT audit with continuous assurance, automated control testing, real-time risk monitoring, and predictive analytics to focus audit resources on emerging risk areas.

assurance-and-compliance.name: IT Audit Independence and Competency
assurance-and-compliance.description: Ensure the IT audit function maintains independence, objectivity, and adequate technical competency to effectively assess complex technology and cybersecurity environments.
keyActivities: Maintain organisational independence of the IT audit function with direct reporting to the audit committee
Ensure IT auditors possess relevant certifications (CISA, CISSP, CRISC) and receive ongoing technical training
Supplement internal IT audit capability with specialised external resources for complex technology assessments
Assess and maintain the competency matrix for IT audit staff covering emerging technologies such as cloud, AI, and blockchain

maturity.basic: IT audit function with defined independence charter and auditors holding relevant professional certifications.
maturity.mature: Dedicated IT audit team with specialised skills in cybersecurity, cloud, and emerging technology, supplemented by co-sourcing arrangements for niche expertise.
maturity.advanced: IT audit centre of excellence with advanced data analytics capability, continuous professional development programme, and thought leadership on technology risk assurance methodologies.

assurance-and-compliance.name: Third-Party Security Assessment
assurance-and-compliance.description: Conduct security assessments of third-party technology service providers, cloud providers, and outsourcing partners to validate compliance with contractual and regulatory security requirements.
keyActivities: Perform risk-based security assessments of critical third-party service providers on an annual basis at minimum
Evaluate third-party SOC 2 Type II reports, ISO 27001 certifications, and conduct supplementary assessments where gaps exist
Assess cloud service provider security posture against BNM cloud risk management requirements
Monitor third-party security performance through KPIs, SLA compliance, and incident notification adherence
Maintain a register of all third-party technology dependencies with risk ratings and assessment schedules

maturity.basic: Annual third-party security assessments using questionnaires and review of certifications for critical service providers.
maturity.mature: Structured third-party security programme with risk-tiered assessment methodologies, continuous monitoring of critical providers, and contractual right-to-audit enforcement.
maturity.advanced: Continuous third-party risk intelligence with automated security rating monitoring, real-time risk alerting, supply chain attack surface mapping, and collaborative security improvement programmes with strategic providers.

assurance-and-compliance.name: Security Awareness Program
assurance-and-compliance.description: Deliver a comprehensive security awareness programme to all staff, contractors, and board members to build a security-conscious culture aligned with BNM RMiT expectations.
keyActivities: Deliver mandatory annual security awareness training covering cyber threats, data protection, and regulatory responsibilities
Provide role-specific training for high-risk roles such as IT administrators, developers, and treasury operations staff
Measure awareness effectiveness through assessments, phishing simulation results, and incident reporting metrics
Maintain training records and ensure completion rates meet compliance thresholds with escalation for non-compliance

maturity.basic: Annual mandatory security awareness training for all staff with completion tracking and basic effectiveness measurement.
maturity.mature: Continuous security awareness programme with monthly micro-learning, role-specific modules, gamification, and behaviour-based metrics informing programme content.
maturity.advanced: Adaptive security awareness with personalised learning paths based on individual risk profiles, real-time teachable moments triggered by user behaviour, and security culture measurement integrated into enterprise risk reporting.

assurance-and-compliance.name: Phishing Simulation
assurance-and-compliance.description: Conduct regular phishing simulation exercises to measure employee susceptibility and reinforce security awareness in recognising and reporting social engineering attacks.
keyActivities: Conduct monthly phishing simulation campaigns with varied scenarios reflecting current threat landscape targeting Malaysian banks
Track click rates, credential submission rates, and reporting rates as key performance indicators
Provide immediate just-in-time training for employees who fall for simulated phishing attempts
Report phishing simulation trends to management and use results to tailor awareness programme content

maturity.basic: Quarterly phishing simulations with basic reporting on click rates and remedial training for repeat offenders.
maturity.mature: Monthly simulations with diverse scenarios (spear-phishing, BEC, smishing), department-level analytics, and progressive difficulty based on employee performance.
maturity.advanced: Continuous phishing resilience testing with AI-generated personalised attack simulations, real-time reporting integration, peer benchmarking, and predictive modelling of organisational phishing risk.

assurance-and-compliance.name: Role-Based Security Training
assurance-and-compliance.description: Deliver specialised security training programmes tailored to specific roles including developers, system administrators, and senior management to address role-specific risk exposure.
keyActivities: Develop and maintain role-based training curricula for developers (secure coding), IT ops (secure configuration), and management (cyber risk oversight)
Deliver secure coding training to all application developers covering OWASP Top 10, API security, and secure SDLC practices
Provide board and senior management with periodic cyber risk briefings and governance training
Track role-based training completion and effectiveness through practical assessments and certification requirements

maturity.basic: Role-based training modules for key technical roles with annual completion requirements and basic assessments.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive role-based programme with hands-on labs, capture-the-flag exercises for technical staff, and board-level cyber simulation exercises.
maturity.advanced: Adaptive role-based training with personalised learning paths, continuous skill assessment, cyber range exercises for incident responders, and executive war-gaming for senior leadership.

assurance-and-compliance.name: Incident Notification Framework
assurance-and-compliance.description: Establish a regulatory incident notification framework ensuring timely and accurate reporting of technology and cybersecurity incidents to BNM within mandated timelines.
keyActivities: Define incident classification criteria aligned with BNM RMiT notification thresholds and severity levels
Establish notification workflows ensuring critical incidents are reported to BNM within mandated timelines (typically 1 hour for critical events)
Maintain pre-approved notification templates and communication channels for BNM incident reporting
Conduct post-incident reporting including root cause analysis and remediation plans as required by BNM

maturity.basic: Documented incident notification procedures with defined BNM reporting thresholds, templates, and designated contact points.
maturity.mature: Streamlined notification workflow with automated severity classification, pre-staged report templates, and rehearsed escalation procedures with designated BNM liaison.
maturity.advanced: Integrated incident reporting platform with automated threshold detection, pre-populated regulatory notification forms, and real-time coordination capabilities with BNM and industry peers.

assurance-and-compliance.name: Technology Change Notification
assurance-and-compliance.description: Notify BNM of significant technology changes, system implementations, and major IT projects as required by RMiT notification provisions.
keyActivities: Maintain a register of technology changes requiring BNM notification such as core system replacements, cloud migrations, and new digital services
Submit technology change notifications to BNM within prescribed timelines with required supporting documentation
Coordinate with business and technology teams to compile impact assessments, risk evaluations, and implementation plans for BNM submissions
Track BNM feedback on notifications and ensure any conditions or observations are addressed prior to go-live

maturity.basic: Manual tracking of technology changes requiring BNM notification with documented submission procedures.
maturity.mature: Centralised change notification register with workflow-driven preparation, review, and submission process with automated timeline tracking and reminder alerts.
maturity.advanced: Integrated regulatory change management platform with automated identification of notifiable changes from project portfolio, pre-populated submission templates, and regulatory feedback tracking.

assurance-and-compliance.name: Outsourcing Notification
assurance-and-compliance.description: Notify BNM of material outsourcing arrangements for technology services and ensure outsourcing agreements meet RMiT requirements for risk management and data protection.
keyActivities: Identify and classify technology outsourcing arrangements that require BNM notification based on materiality and criticality
Submit outsourcing notifications to BNM with comprehensive risk assessments, due diligence reports, and contractual terms
Ensure outsourcing agreements include BNM-required provisions for audit rights, data sovereignty, exit management, and business continuity
Monitor outsourced service provider performance and report material changes or incidents to BNM as required

maturity.basic: Manual identification and notification of material outsourcing arrangements with documented BNM submission procedures.
maturity.mature: Centralised outsourcing register with risk classification, automated notification tracking, and contractual compliance monitoring against BNM RMiT requirements.
maturity.advanced: Integrated third-party risk and outsourcing governance platform with automated materiality assessment, continuous provider monitoring, and proactive regulatory engagement on outsourcing arrangements.

assurance-and-compliance.name: Regulatory Examination Response
assurance-and-compliance.description: Manage responses to BNM regulatory examinations, on-site inspections, and supervisory findings with structured tracking and timely remediation of identified issues.
keyActivities: Establish a coordination framework for BNM regulatory examinations including document preparation, interview scheduling, and evidence management
Track all regulatory examination findings and supervisory observations in a centralised remediation tracker
Develop and execute remediation action plans with defined timelines, owners, and milestones agreed with BNM
Report remediation progress to board audit committee and BNM within prescribed timelines

maturity.basic: Documented examination response procedures with centralised tracking of findings and remediation actions.
maturity.mature: Structured examination management programme with dedicated coordination function, evidence repository, automated remediation tracking, and periodic progress reporting to board.
maturity.advanced: Proactive regulatory engagement with predictive examination readiness assessments, continuous compliance monitoring reducing examination findings, and data-driven remediation prioritisation.

assurance-and-compliance.name: Annual Technology Risk Reporting
assurance-and-compliance.description: Prepare and submit annual technology risk reports to BNM summarising the institution's technology risk profile, key risk indicators, and control effectiveness.
keyActivities: Compile annual technology risk report covering risk profile, incident trends, audit findings, and control effectiveness metrics
Include assessment of emerging technology risks such as AI, cloud concentration, and supply chain dependencies
Obtain board or board risk committee endorsement of the annual technology risk report prior to BNM submission
Benchmark technology risk metrics against industry standards and prior-year performance to demonstrate trend analysis

maturity.basic: Annual technology risk report compiled manually with key metrics and submitted to BNM within prescribed timelines.
maturity.mature: Structured annual reporting with automated data aggregation from GRC platforms, trend analysis, peer benchmarking, and board-endorsed submission.
maturity.advanced: Continuous technology risk reporting capability with real-time dashboards, automated annual report generation, predictive risk analytics, and proactive identification of emerging risk themes for BNM engagement.

assurance-and-compliance.name: RMiT Self-Assessment
assurance-and-compliance.description: Conduct periodic self-assessments against BNM RMiT requirements to identify compliance gaps, track remediation progress, and demonstrate regulatory compliance maturity.
keyActivities: Perform annual self-assessment against all applicable BNM RMiT policy documents and clauses
Map existing controls to RMiT requirements and identify gaps with risk-rated remediation priorities
Track self-assessment findings in a centralised compliance register with assigned owners and target dates
Report self-assessment results and remediation progress to board risk committee and designated BNM liaison

maturity.basic: Annual RMiT self-assessment conducted via spreadsheets with gap identification and basic remediation tracking.
maturity.mature: Structured self-assessment programme with GRC platform support, evidence-linked control mapping, automated gap analysis, and quarterly remediation progress reporting.
maturity.advanced: Continuous compliance monitoring with automated control testing against RMiT requirements, real-time compliance dashboards, predictive gap identification, and integration with regulatory change management.

assurance-and-compliance.name: Gap Remediation Tracking
assurance-and-compliance.description: Track and manage the remediation of identified compliance gaps from RMiT self-assessments, regulatory examinations, and audit findings through to verified closure.
keyActivities: Maintain a centralised remediation register consolidating gaps from self-assessments, audits, regulatory examinations, and penetration tests
Define remediation action plans with clear owners, milestones, resource requirements, and target completion dates
Implement escalation procedures for overdue remediation items with reporting to senior management and board audit committee
Verify remediation effectiveness through independent validation before closing items in the compliance register
Report remediation status and trends to BNM as required and during regulatory examinations

maturity.basic: Centralised remediation tracker with defined owners, target dates, and periodic status reporting to management.
maturity.mature: GRC-platform-managed remediation with automated status tracking, escalation workflows, evidence management, and integrated reporting across audit, risk, and compliance functions.
maturity.advanced: Intelligent remediation management with risk-based prioritisation, predictive completion analysis, automated evidence validation, and continuous monitoring to detect control regression after remediation.
